title: __version__ not re-exported from camtasia.__init__
description: camtasia/__init__.py does not import or re-export __version__ from camtasia.version. Users expect `import camtasia; camtasia.__version__` to work (PEP 396 convention). Currently they must use `from camtasia.version import __version__`. The __all__ list also omits __version__.
recommendation: Add `from camtasia.version import __version__` and include `__version__` in __all__.

title: Missing License trove classifier in pyproject.toml
description: The classifiers list does not include a License classifier (e.g. 'License :: OSI Approved :: MIT License'). While pyproject.toml declares license = 'MIT', PyPI uses classifiers for filtering and display. Without it, the package won't appear in license-filtered searches on PyPI.
recommendation: Add 'License :: OSI Approved :: MIT License' to the classifiers list.

title: docopt-subcommands dependency has no version pin
description: In [project.optional-dependencies] cli, 'docopt-subcommands' has no version constraint. This is a transitive dependency on the unmaintained 'docopt' package (last release 2014). A major version bump could break the CLI. The exit-codes dependency is properly pinned.
evidence: cli = ["exit-codes>=1.3.0,<2", "docopt-subcommands"] — no version specifier on docopt-subcommands
recommendation: Pin to 'docopt-subcommands>=4.0,<5' or consider migrating to click/typer which are actively maintained.

title: sdist includes ~10MB of test fixtures, bloating PyPI upload
description: MANIFEST.in includes 'recursive-include tests/fixtures *' which pulls in 5MB empty.wav, 2.7MB test_project_a.tscproj, and other large binary fixtures totaling ~10MB uncompressed. The sdist is 1.3MB compressed but these fixtures are unnecessary for downstream users. PyPI has a 100MB limit but this is wasteful and slows pip install from sdist.
evidence: ls -lh tests/fixtures/ shows: empty.wav 5.0M, test_project_a.tscproj 2.7M, test_project_b.tscproj 1.0M, etc. Total ~10.8MB. tar tzf dist/*.tar.gz | grep fixtures confirms all are included.
recommendation: Either exclude large fixtures from MANIFEST.in (users can clone the repo to run tests) or add a size budget check in CI. Consider 'recursive-include tests *.py' only and documenting that fixtures require a git clone.

title: examples/ directory not included in sdist
description: The top-level examples/ directory (8 files including runnable Python scripts and a test) is not included in the sdist. Users installing from source won't have access to the examples. MANIFEST.in has no rule for examples/.
evidence: tar tzf dist/*.tar.gz | grep examples shows only docs/examples/index.md, not the top-level examples/ directory

title: release.yml workflow not included in sdist
description: MANIFEST.in does not explicitly include .github/ files. Only tests.yml and docs.yml appear in the sdist (likely auto-included by setuptools via SOURCES.txt). The release.yml workflow is missing, so the sdist doesn't contain the full CI/CD configuration.
evidence: tar tzf dist/*.tar.gz | grep release returns empty. tar tzf dist/*.tar.gz | grep github shows only docs.yml and tests.yml.
recommendation: Add 'recursive-include .github *.yml *.md' to MANIFEST.in if you want the full project in the sdist, or document that the sdist is intentionally minimal.

title: Version is duplicated between pyproject.toml and version.py with no single-source-of-truth mechanism
description: The version '0.1.0' is hardcoded in both pyproject.toml (version = "0.1.0") and src/camtasia/version.py (__version__ = "0.1.0"). The bumpversion tool in [dev] extras suggests automated bumping, but there's no pyproject.toml dynamic version configuration or setuptools-scm to keep them in sync. A release where one is bumped but not the other would ship mismatched metadata.
evidence: pyproject.toml: version = "0.1.0"; src/camtasia/version.py: __version__ = "0.1.0". No [project] dynamic = ["version"] declaration.
recommendation: Use setuptools dynamic version: add dynamic = ["version"] to [project], remove the static version, and add [tool.setuptools.dynamic] version = {attr = "camtasia.version.__version__"}. Or use setuptools-scm for git-tag-based versioning.

title: pyproject.toml uses new-style license field (PEP 639) which may not render on older pip versions
description: pyproject.toml uses license = "MIT" (PEP 639 SPDX expression) rather than the legacy license = {text = "MIT"} format. This is correct for modern tooling but older pip versions (<24) may not display the license correctly. Combined with the missing License classifier (REV-packaging-002), license visibility on PyPI could be reduced.
recommendation: This is fine for modern tooling. Adding the License classifier (REV-packaging-002) provides backward-compatible visibility.

title: Package name 'pycamtasia' differs from import name 'camtasia'
description: The PyPI package name is 'pycamtasia' but the import name is 'camtasia' (pip install pycamtasia; import camtasia). This is a common pattern but can confuse users. The README should clearly document this. The import name 'camtasia' could also conflict with any future official TechSmith package.
evidence: name = "pycamtasia" in pyproject.toml; [tool.setuptools.packages.find] where = ["src"] finds 'camtasia' package
recommendation: Ensure README prominently shows 'pip install pycamtasia' followed by 'import camtasia'. Consider adding a note about the naming convention.

title: pymediainfo optional dependency has no version pin
description: In [project.optional-dependencies] media, 'pymediainfo' has no version constraint. While D-008 documents graceful degradation, a breaking change in pymediainfo's API could cause silent failures rather than clean fallback.
recommendation: Pin to 'pymediainfo>=6.0' or similar lower bound to ensure API compatibility.

title: Test dependencies include tools that could be in [dev] instead
description: The [test] optional-dependencies group includes 'mypy' and 'ruff' which are linting/type-checking tools, not test dependencies. The [dev] group already has 'ruff'. This means 'pip install pycamtasia[test]' installs more than needed to run pytest.
evidence: test = ["pytest", "pytest-cov", "hypothesis", "pytest-xdist", "pytest-timeout", "mypy", "jsonschema", "ruff"] — mypy and ruff are lint tools, not test runners
recommendation: Move mypy and ruff to [dev] only. The CI workflow can install [test,dev] explicitly.

title: CI test matrix does not test on macOS or Windows despite OS Independent classifier
description: The classifiers include 'Operating System :: OS Independent' and the library manipulates file paths for .cmproj/.tscproj bundles. However, the CI matrix (tests.yml) only runs on ubuntu-latest. Path handling differences on Windows (backslashes) could cause issues that aren't caught.
evidence: runs-on: ubuntu-latest with no OS matrix. Classifier: 'Operating System :: OS Independent'.
recommendation: Add at least macos-latest to the CI matrix since Camtasia is primarily a macOS/Windows application. Consider windows-latest too.
